title: Airborne Transmission of Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza A/H5N1 Between Ferrets Via Respiratory Droplets Following Sequential Passage
abstract: Highly pathogenic avian influenza A/H5N1 viruses pose a significant pandemic threat but have not demonstrated sustained human-to-human transmission. Here we show that serial passage of A/Indonesia/5/2005 (H5N1) in ferrets generates variants capable of airborne transmission via respiratory droplets. After ten
